question.text: Under the SEBI Delisting of Equity Shares Regulations, 2021, what type of resolution is required from shareholders for voluntary delisting?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI Delisting Regulations, 2021 require a special resolution — i.e., approval by at least 2/3 majority of shareholders — for voluntary delisting. This higher threshold protects minority shareholders from being forced out without adequate consent. **The Trap:** "Ordinary resolution" sounds plausible because many corporate actions require only a simple majority, but delisting permanently removes trading access and hence demands a higher bar. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) also touch on corporate actions and shareholder approvals. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify the resolution type in the notice of the general meeting — a voluntary delisting approved by only an ordinary resolution is invalid and can be challenged.
